world_date: Month of the Iron Thaw, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord

recent_events.text: Clan Arkenor raiders ambushed a Wayfarer patrol at the Greyveil crossing. Two elves slain, one taken. Elowen's silence finally breaks — she has summoned the War-Readers.
A reformer leader, Kessa Stonebraid, smuggled herself into the sealed lower district. She has not returned. Her torch was found at the threshold, still burning.
King Roderic Thorne II appeared at court in a litter — pale, barely speaking. His eldest son Aldric publicly called for a Regency Council. The court erupted.
Three Tidefall warships entered Eldoria harbor under 'courtesy visit' pretense. They have not left. Dockworkers report weapons being offloaded after dark.
A second orc caravan — Groth-flagged — was found dead. Unlike the Gilgeth massacre, these bodies bore no blackbloom traces. Something else killed them.

active_tensions.description: Arkenor's killing of Wayfarer scouts has forced Elowen's hand. The War-Readers convene. Open conflict is no longer unlikely — it is scheduled.
Kessa Stonebraid's disappearance into the sealed district has made her a martyr symbol. Reformers are arming. Ironmaul's silence is becoming a fuse.
Tidefall warships occupy Eldoria harbor. Aldric's Regency push divides the court — half see Tidefall's ships as leverage, half as invasion. No one blinks.
Groth caravan deaths without blackbloom signature suggests a third party is escalating the conflict. Both orc cities now mobilizing war-bands, blaming each other.

character_updates.name: Queen Lythara the Veiled
character_updates.status: Ordered Arkenor's raid — but Clan Nythariel is whispering she did not sanction the killing. A fracture, or theater?

character_updates.name: Sovereign Elowen Silverleaf
character_updates.status: War-Readers summoned. The pacifist Sovereign has not wept. Those close to her say that is the most frightening thing they have ever seen.

character_updates.name: Thane Ulric Ironmaul
character_updates.status: Has posted armed guards at the sealed threshold. Ordered Kessa Stonebraid declared 'lost to the deep.' Reformers call it murder by decree.

character_updates.name: King Roderic Thorne II
character_updates.status: Appears lucid in private, but performs frailty in public. The question is whether the performance is protection — or trap.

character_updates.name: Prince Aldric Thorne
character_updates.status: Newly emerged. Ambitious, sharp-tongued, and already courting Tidefall's Admiral. His younger brother Caeris has gone very quiet.

character_updates.name: Pirate King Rowen
character_updates.status: His ship docked briefly at a Tidefall outer isle. He met with someone in a hooded cloak for under an hour. Then vanished south.

faction_morale.reason: Arkenor's raid was bold. Fear-unity holds, but Nythariel's murmuring introduces first real internal friction.
Kessa Stonebraid's disappearance has inflamed reformers. Armed gatherings reported in upper districts. Ironmaul's grip slipping.
Grief has become purpose. The Wayfarer killing unified a fractured pacifist faction — quiet anger is more dangerous than loud.
Tidefall ships in harbor and open succession struggle have shattered any pretense of stability. Merchants are moving coin east.
Warships in Eldoria harbor without a sword drawn. The Admiral calls it diplomacy. Her officers call it conquest. Both are right.
Second massacre without a clear culprit has both cities mobilizing blindly. War-bands on the Rock Plains will find each other before they find the truth.
Village unrest growing around Stonebreak. Three farmers attempted to destroy a standing stone. They were found unconscious a mile away, unharmed but changed.
Faction chaos means every party needs couriers who can cross the passes. Glass orders up 40%. Three new contracts signed in the dark.

whispers.text: Kessa Stonebraid is not dead. Someone inside the sealed district is sliding food under the threshold door. Ironmaul's own guard is doing it.
The hooded figure Rowen met on the Tidefall isle wore Twin Cities court rings. Two rings — one for each city. That means the niece.
The Groth caravan wasn't killed by orcs or blackbloom. The wounds match Gloomspire glass-blades. The Gnomes are not just watching anymore.
Elowen's War-Readers didn't just read the omens. They read Lythara's name in them — written there before the raid ever happened.

weather_and_omens.condition: A second moon-shadow appeared on three consecutive nights — no astronomical cause found. Clan Velorn survivors will not go outside after dusk.
The feverish warmth in the deep stone has intensified. Ice in the lower water-channels is melting upward. Miners say the heat has a pulse.
The three descended Frostwarden elders have not returned to their aeries. They stand in the mountain foothills, facing south, unmoving, for days.
Dust storms have taken on a faint crimson tint. Vilefin Goblin scouts who did return speak of shapes moving beneath the dust that leave no tracks.
Green light observed rising from the standing stones on moonless nights. The dead calm persists. A druid novice was found at the stones, speaking in a language no one recognizes.

absorbed_lore: Clan Arkenor killed two Wayfarer elves and took one captive — Elowen has summoned War-Readers, ending Glenhaven's pacifist posture.
Reformer leader Kessa Stonebraid entered Lostfeld's sealed lower district and vanished; Ironmaul declared her 'lost to the deep,' inflaming reformers.
King Roderic may be performing his frailty deliberately; eldest son Aldric is openly courting Tidefall and pushing for a Regency Council.
A second orc caravan — Groth-flagged — was found dead with wounds consistent with Gloomspire glass-blades, not blackbloom.
Tidefall warships are docked in Eldoria harbor under 'courtesy visit' pretense; weapons offloaded after dark.
Pirate King Rowen met a hooded Twin Cities court figure (suspected: King Roderic's niece) on a Tidefall outer isle.
The three Frostwarden Ice Dragon elders remain motionless in the mountain foothills, facing south — no movement in weeks.
Gloomspire Gnomes appear to have broken from passive observation; glass-blade wounds on Groth caravan suggest active interference.
A druid novice was found at Stonebreak standing stones speaking an unrecognized language; green light rising from stones on moonless nights.
Lostfeld stone heat now has a perceived rhythmic pulse; ice melting upward in lower water-channels.
